title: NYC Film Permits Data
text: nycsets packages film, television, production permits issued New York City Mayor’s Office Media Entertainment (MOME). provides one main permits table three long companion tables link permit affected community boards, NYPD precincts, ZIP codes.

title: NYC Film Permits Data
text: can install development version nycsets GitHub :

title: NYC Film Permits Data
text: Top production categories borough: Permits per day time:  map streets held parking one busy permit day, 2024-09-12:  Top ten ZIP codes permit count:
code: nyc_film_permits_df |>   filter(!is.na(borough)) |>   count(borough, category) |>   pivot_wider(     names_from = borough,     values_from = n,     values_fill = 0   ) |>   arrange(desc(Manhattan)) #> # A tibble: 9 × 6 #>   category          Bronx Brooklyn Manhattan Queens `Staten Island` #>   <chr>             <int>    <int>     <int>  <int>           <int> #> 1 Television          314     2404      2853   1680              50 #> 2 Theater               5      878      2431      0               0 #> 3 Film                 74      881      1076    302              70 #> 4 Commercial           58      753       968    136               9 #> 5 Still Photography     2      321       715     33               1 #> 6 WEB                  14      309       480     37               6 #> 7 Documentary           2       42        89     11               8 #> 8 Student               6       26        49     18               0 #> 9 Music Video           1       31        29      5               0 nyc_film_permits_df |>   filter(!is.na(start_date_time)) |>   mutate(date = as.Date(start_date_time)) |>   count(date) |>   ggplot(aes(date, n)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +   geom_smooth(se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "Permits issued",     title = "NYC film permits per day",     caption = "Source: NYC MOME via NYC OpenData"   ) +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")' day <- as.Date("2024-09-12") day_permits <- nyc_film_permits_df |>   filter(     as.Date(start_date_time, tz = "America/New_York") == day,     borough == "Manhattan"   ) day_geo <- nyc_film_permit_parking_sf |>   filter(event_id %in% day_permits$event_id) ggplot(day_geo) +   geom_sf(colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.6) +   coord_sf(crs = 2263) +   labs(     title = "Film-permit parking holds in Manhattan",     subtitle = format(day, "%B %e, %Y"),     caption = "Source: NYC MOME + NYC LION via NYC OpenData"   ) +   theme_void() nyc_film_permit_zips_df |>   count(zip_code, sort = TRUE) |>   slice_head(n = 10) #> # A tibble: 10 × 2 #>    zip_code     n #>    <chr>    <int> #>  1 11222     2533 #>  2 11101     1338 #>  3 10036     1111 #>  4 10019     1020 #>  5 10001      975 #>  6 10013      950 #>  7 10023      924 #>  8 10003      877 #>  9 10011      603 #> 10 11201      600

what: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
title: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
text: Long-format companion table parsing free-text parking_held column nyc_film_permits_df one row per "MAIN STREET -STREET -STREET" stretch.

title: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
text: tibble 58,172 rows 6 columns: event_id Integer. Permit identifier. Joins nyc_film_permits_df$event_id. segment_seq Integer. Ordinal position stretch within original comma-separated parking_held string permit. Together event_id, uniquely identifies parsed stretch. main_street Character. street whose parking held. from_street Character. Cross street naming one end stretch. to_street Character. Cross street naming end stretch. matched Logical. TRUE geometry successfully resolved LION; see nyc_film_permit_parking_sf.

title: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
text: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/City-Government/Film-Permits/tg4x-b46p

title: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
text: Approximately 87% parsed stretches successfully geolocate one LION street segments. unmatched 13% mostly typos, renamed streets alt-name table, multi-direction data-entry oddities "EAST WEST 44 STREET", highway service-road / direction codes "CLEARVIEW EXPRESSWAY SR SB". Many parsed stretches span one block (e.g., "AMSTERDAM AVENUE WEST 62 STREET WEST 65 STREET"). Geolocation performed shortest path along per-(main-street, borough) subgraph LION, one parsed stretch can produce multiple rows companion sf object.

title: Film permits to parsed parking-held street stretches (long) — nyc_film_permit_parking_df
text: Kieran Healy

title: Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
text: Spatial companion nyc_film_permit_parking_df containing LION street segments matched parsed parking-held stretch. One row per (event_id, segment_seq, segment_id) triple; single parsed stretch spanning multiple blocks produces several rows. EPSG:2263, NAD83 / New York Long Island (ftUS).

title: Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
text: simple feature collection 97,182 rows 10 columns: event_id Integer. Permit identifier. Joins nyc_film_permits_df$event_id. segment_seq Integer. Ordinal position parsed stretch within permit's parking_held string. Joins nyc_film_permit_parking_df (event_id, segment_seq). main_street Character. street whose parking held, appeared source text. segment_id Character. LION seven-digit segment identifier. physical_id Integer. LION physical-segment ID. Multiple LION rows can share physical_id; group column recover one row per block face. lion_street Character. canonical LION street name matched segment. l_boro, r_boro Integer. LION borough code left / right side segment: 1 Manhattan, 2 Bronx, 3 Brooklyn, 4 Queens, 5 Staten Island. l_zip, r_zip Character. ZIP code left / right side. SHAPE Linestring / multilinestring geometry EPSG:2263.

title: Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
text: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/City-Government/Film-Permits/tg4x-b46p NYC Department City Planning LION https://www.nyc.gov/site/planning/data-maps/open-data.page.

title: Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
text: Geometry taken directly nyclion::nyc_lion_sf. Matching uses street-name normalizer (ordinal-word expansion, abbreviation expansion, compass-letter expansion) plus LION alt-names table, shortest-path search per-(main-street, borough) subgraph LION segments. 87% parsed parking-held stretches resolve least one LION segment. LION subdivides block-face one sub-segments minor nodes (driveways, internal breakpoints). recover one row per block face, group dissolve physical_id.

title: Geolocated film-permit parking stretches — nyc_film_permit_parking_sf
text: Kieran Healy

title: NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
text: Permits issued New York City Mayor's Office Media Entertainment (MOME) film, television, productions asserting exclusive use city property (streets, sidewalks, parks, DCAS facilities). One row per permit (event).

title: NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
text: tibble 17,177 rows 13 columns: event_id Integer. Auto-generated unique permit identifier. event_type Character. Type activity covered permit: "Shooting Permit", "Rigging Permit", "Theater Load Load Outs", "DCAS Prep/Shoot/Wrap Permit". start_date_time POSIXct (America/New_York). Scheduled start permitted activity. May NA source row omits start time. end_date_time POSIXct (America/New_York). Scheduled completion permitted activity. May NA. entered_on POSIXct (America/New_York). Time permit request submitted MOME. parking_held Character. Free-text description cross streets intersections parking held advance filming. Multiple segments separated commas. degree street impact may one truck length, two truck lengths, quarter block, half block, full block. borough Character. First borough activity day: "Manhattan", "Brooklyn", "Queens", "Bronx", "Staten Island". NA borough applies (e.g., aerial activity). community_boards Character. Comma-separated list community boards affected permit. See nyc_film_permit_boards_df long-format companion one row per (event_id, board). police_precincts Character. Comma-separated list NYPD precincts affected. See nyc_film_permit_precincts_df long companion. category Character. Production category selected applicant: "Commercial", "Documentary", "Film", "Music Video", "Still Photography", "Student", "Television", "Theater", "WEB". sub_category_name Character. specific production description (e.g., "Episodic series", "Feature", "News", "PSA"). country Character. Project origin country. zip_codes Character. Comma-separated list ZIP codes production activity. See nyc_film_permit_zips_df long companion.

title: NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
text: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/City-Government/Film-Permits/tg4x-b46p

title: NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
text: Source data "Film Permits" dataset published NYC OpenData Mayor's Office Media Entertainment. permits table approved permits; status therefore retained column. Date fields parsed source MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM:SS /PM format POSIXct America/New_York time zone. constant EventAgency column (always MOME) dropped.

title: NYC film permits — nyc_film_permits_df
text: Kieran Healy
